metadata.title: Western US Motorhome & Adventure Bike Navigation Resources
metadata.description: Comprehensive database of mapping tools, camping resources, navigation utilities, and travel planning tools for motorhome and adventure motorcycle travel in the Western United States. Consolidated from multiple sources with 70+ resources across 10 categories.

vehicleProfile.notes: Use RV/truck routing tools and verify every low-clearance warning against posted signs, state DOT data, and local agency information.

fields.id: Stable unique key for resource identification
fields.name: Resource name/title
fields.type: Resource type (App, Web, Hardware, API, PDF, etc.)
fields.platforms: Supported platforms (Web, iOS, Android, Print, Dedicated Device)
fields.states: US state coverage or 'US' for nationwide
fields.geoFormats: Relevant geospatial formats (GPX, KML, GeoPDF, etc.)

resources.name: Low Clearance Map + HeadRoom
resources.description: Largest publicly available low-clearance dataset with 24,000+ verified clearances. Includes HeadRoom mobile app with voice-guided turn-by-turn navigation and Sentry Mode hazard monitoring. Directional metadata reduces false alarms.
resources.bestFor: Primary height-aware route planning for 12ft motorhome
resources.notes: Essential for a 12ft rig. Always add 6 inches to your actual height when entering dimensions.

resources.name: RV LIFE Trip Wizard + RV LIFE App
resources.description: End-to-end RV trip planner + RV-safe GPS. Build routes in Trip Wizard (web), then navigate turn-by-turn via the RV LIFE app using your rig's height, weight, and length. Avoids low bridges, steep grades, and propane-restricted tunnels. Includes 20,000+ campground reviews and 57,000+ POIs.
features: Custom RV dimension profiles (height, weight, length)
Good Sam, Escapees, Passport America membership ranking
resources.bestFor: Full-trip planning from home + safe daily navigation on the road
resources.notes: Good companion to Low Clearance Map. Plan on desktop, navigate on phone.

resources.name: Sygic Truck & RV GPS Navigation
resources.cost: $69.99 Truck Premium / $99.99 with CarPlay+Android Auto (lifetime, North America)
resources.description: Plans routes excluding roads not suitable for your RV based on vehicle dimensions, weight, and cargo type. Includes offline maps with 3D buildings and landmarks. Supports CarPlay and Android Auto integration. Lifetime license with free map updates.
features: Dynamic lane assistant and voice guidance
Speed limit alerts and camera warnings (add-on)
Thousands of truck-related POIs and fuel stations
resources.bestFor: Set-it-and-forget-it offline motorhome routing with CarPlay integration
resources.notes: Lifetime pricing is per platform unless purchased via e-shop after March 2022; add-ons (traffic, speed cameras) cost extra.

resources.name: CoPilot RV (Trimble Maps)
resources.description: Premium offline RV navigation with robust vehicle dimension profiles. Generates RV-specific routes that avoid low bridges, weight-restricted roads, and propane-prohibited tunnels. Works fully offline after map download.
resources.bestFor: Offline RV navigation with robust vehicle dimension safety
resources.notes: No trip planning web interface; navigation-only app. Good redundancy against dedicated Garmin or RV LIFE route plan.

resources.name: Garmin RV GPS (890/895/1095 Series)
resources.description: Dedicated RV GPS programmable for height, width, length, and weight. Automatically routes around over-limit structures including low bridges, narrow roads, and weight-restricted bridges. Many experienced RVers consider this essential.
resources.bestFor: Dedicated always-on dashboard navigation for motorhome
resources.notes: Many experienced RVers consider this essential. 895/1095 are current models replacing 890. Keep maps updated; still obey signage and road restrictions.

resources.name: Rand McNally Motor Carriers Road Atlas
resources.description: The trucker's bible - comprehensive road atlas with all low clearances listed by state. Works when electronics fail. Large spiral-bound version available. Multiple experienced RVers recommend this as essential backup.
features: All low clearances listed by state
resources.bestFor: Backup reference and pre-trip planning when electronics fail
resources.notes: Get the large spiral-bound version. Essential backup for when electronics fail.

resources.name: Gaia GPS
resources.description: All-purpose outdoor GPS with excellent public/private land boundary overlays, National Geographic topo maps, and 15+ map layer options. Strong balance of features for overlanding, hiking, and dispersed camping research. The top choice for overlanding in the Western US.
resources.bestFor: Primary adventure bike navigation and dispersed campsite scouting
resources.notes: Download entire states before departing. Integrates with Trails Offroad for 2,400+ curated routes.

resources.name: onX Offroad
resources.description: 650K+ miles of motorized trails with public land boundaries, trail difficulty ratings, open/close dates, and dispersed camping layer. Offline maps with GPS navigation. Official navigation app of the BDR. Includes cell coverage layers and wildfire monitoring.
resources.bestFor: Adventure bike trail finding, land ownership verification, and dispersed camping identification
resources.notes: Pair with Gaia GPS for maximum coverage. The Dispersed Camping Layer uses USFS verified data.

resources.name: Trails Offroad
resources.description: 2,400+ hand-curated off-road routes with detailed trip guides, difficulty ratings, and GPX downloads. Routes sourced from experienced off-road experts. Integrates directly with Gaia GPS for one-click offline use. Great for CA with ~200 tracks.
resources.bestFor: Finding pre-scouted adventure bike routes in Western US
resources.notes: Connect your Gaia GPS account for seamless route import.

resources.name: CalTopo
resources.description: Advanced topographic mapping tool with public land overlays that color-code BLM, Forest Service, and other federal lands. Excellent for detailed trip planning, custom map creation, and GPX route building. Used by SAR teams and serious backcountry travelers.
resources.bestFor: Detailed topographic trip planning and custom map creation
resources.notes: Great for planning adventure bike routes with terrain detail. Steep learning curve; overkill for casual navigation.

resources.name: Avenza Maps
resources.cost: Free app; individual maps from $0 (USFS free) to ~$10+
resources.description: Mobile app for viewing georeferenced PDF maps with GPS positioning. The official distribution platform for USFS Motor Vehicle Use Maps, NPS maps, and USGS topo maps. Shows your real-time location on downloaded PDF maps. Extremely low battery drain.
features: Map store with thousands of free maps
resources.bestFor: Viewing official USFS and BLM georeferenced PDF maps with GPS location in the field
resources.notes: Essential companion to USFS MVUMs. Search for 'MVUM' or specific ranger districts in the map store. All MVUM maps are free.

resources.name: USFS Motor Vehicle Use Maps (MVUM)
resources.description: Official free maps showing designated roads, trails, and areas open to motorized travel on all National Forests and Grasslands. Available as downloadable geospatial PDFs compatible with Avenza Maps app. Shows vehicle class restrictions, seasonal closures, and dispersed camping corridors.
features: Free download from each forest website
resources.bestFor: Legal compliance - knowing where motorized travel is permitted in National Forests
resources.notes: Download MVUMs for every National Forest along your route. If a route is not on the MVUM it is NOT open to motor vehicle travel. Use alongside a topo map for navigation.

resources.name: BLM National Data Map / Navigator
resources.description: Official BLM online map tool showing land boundaries, land status, roads, and recreation sites across all BLM-managed lands. The official source for confirming BLM land ownership and boundaries. Can be clunky but is the definitive source for BLM land verification.
resources.bestFor: Confirming BLM land boundaries and finding official BLM recreation sites
resources.notes: Use alongside onX or Gaia GPS for better interface. Definitive source for BLM land verification.

resources.name: BLM Georeferenced PDF Maps
resources.description: State-specific digital maps from BLM compatible with GPS-enabled devices. Show land boundaries, roads, trails, and topography. Can be used with Avenza Maps for real-time GPS location overlay. BLM library of georeferenced PDF maps covering campgrounds, recreation areas, wilderness areas, OHV areas, back-country byways, and special management areas.
features: Covers campground maps, OHV areas, wilderness, byways
Maps updated regularly by district offices
resources.bestFor: Offline BLM land navigation in areas without cell service
resources.notes: Download maps for AZ, CA, CO, ID, MT, NV, NM, OR, UT, WA, WY before your trip. Coverage and detail vary by district.

resources.name: USFS Interactive Visitor Map
resources.description: Interactive map to locate all campgrounds, trails, and forest roads catalogued in National Forests. Find recreation sites, visitor centers, trailheads, and points of interest. USFS visitor map for national forests, roads, trails, recreation sites, and related visitor information.
resources.bestFor: Pre-trip research on National Forest campgrounds and trail access
resources.notes: Use for pre-trip research then download specific MVUMs for field navigation. Pair with the official MVUM before driving motorized routes.

resources.name: Recreation.gov
resources.description: Official federal reservation system for campgrounds in National Parks, National Forests, BLM, Army Corps of Engineers, and other federal lands. Book campsites, permits, and day-use passes. Primary reservation and discovery portal for many federal campgrounds, permits, tours, and passes.
resources.bestFor: Reserving developed campgrounds in National Parks and Forests
resources.notes: Popular campgrounds book 6 months in advance. Check early for desired dates. Use to check official booking windows, site lengths, closures, and fees.

resources.name: iOverlander
resources.description: Free crowdsourced database of campgrounds, free camping, water, dump stations, propane, and overlander services worldwide. Popular among overlanders and international travelers. Community-driven app designed by overlanders and travelers for finding wild camping spots, water refill stations, dump stations, and off-grid resources.
features: Camp condition and road access notes
resources.bestFor: Finding free camping and overlander services along your route
resources.notes: Web version gives full access with no account. Treat listings as lead generators - always verify with recent comments. Excellent for adventure travel and motorhome boondocking.

resources.name: Campendium
resources.description: Detailed campground review platform with cell signal information, price data, and campground tips from real users. Covers RV parks, public lands, free camping, overnight parking, and dump stations. RV-focused campground review platform beloved by digital nomads and full-timers. Uniquely includes cell signal strength reports per site.
resources.bestFor: Researching campground quality, cell coverage, and real-world conditions
resources.notes: Pro plan adds cell coverage reports and offline access. Very strong for Western US coverage. No subscription required.

resources.name: The Dyrt
resources.description: Claims largest campground database in the US. PRO adds USFS and BLM Map Layers showing free camping areas, with downloadable offline map regions. Strong photo and review coverage. Camping app with public/private campgrounds, free-camping filters, reviews, and paid offline features.
resources.bestFor: Discovering campgrounds with visual content and free camping layers
resources.notes: PRO subscription needed for the BLM/USFS map layers that make it most useful for dispersed camping. Use BLM/USFS overlays as leads, then verify official rules.

resources.name: FreeCampsites.net
resources.description: No-frills database of free and low-cost camping locations across the US with user reviews, photos, and GPS coordinates. Color-coded maps distinguish free sites from permit and fee sites. No account required. Data skews toward established dispersed spots on BLM and National Forest land.
resources.bestFor: Desktop/tablet pre-trip research for free camping on public land
resources.notes: Best for pre-trip planning from desktop. Data skews toward established dispersed spots on BLM and National Forest land.

resources.name: AllStays Camp & RV
resources.description: Comprehensive directory of campgrounds, RV parks, rest stops, Walmart lots, dump stations, and low clearance alerts. Allows rig height entry. One of the most recommended all-in-one RV apps. The most comprehensive RV infrastructure map app, covering campgrounds, RV parks, truck stops, rest areas, Walmart/Cracker Barrel overnight parking, dump stations, propane fill stations, and weigh stations.
resources.bestFor: All-in-one big-picture trip planning with diverse stop types
resources.notes: One-time purchase, no subscription. iOS only for mobile app. Highly recommended by full-time RVers. Good supplemental road-hazard and services database.

resources.name: onX Maps (BLM Land Maps)
resources.description: Interactive BLM land maps showing 247.3 million acres of public lands with yellow shading. Includes land ownership boundaries, recreation information, and camping regulations by area. BLM lands comprise 247.3 million acres primarily in western states.
resources.bestFor: Verifying you are on public (BLM) land before setting up camp
resources.notes: Yellow shading on map denotes BLM land. 247.3 million acres primarily in western states.

resources.name: Dispersed App
resources.description: Maps out free camping spots on BLM, USFS, and other public lands. Filter by location, see user reviews, and find GPS coordinates to verified campsites. Save locations for offline use. Offline save feature is critical for BLM land where cell service is nonexistent.
resources.bestFor: Quick discovery of verified dispersed camping spots
resources.notes: Offline save feature is critical for BLM land where cell service is nonexistent.

resources.name: RVParky
resources.description: Free app with no subscription covering campgrounds, overnight parking spots, dump stations, and rest areas. Particularly strong for stops that don't show in recreation databases: city parks, fairgrounds, municipal lots. Most recommended free camping app among experienced RVers for en-route stops.
resources.bestFor: Finding overnight stops in unfamiliar towns, especially late in the day
resources.notes: Free, with no subscription and no paywall.

resources.name: Harvest Hosts
resources.description: Unique overnight RV stays at farms, wineries, breweries, and attractions. Self-contained RVs only. Great for interesting stopover locations between public land camping. Membership network for self-contained RV overnight stays at hosts such as farms, wineries, breweries, and attractions.
features: Map layers: satellite, cell coverage, weather
resources.bestFor: Interesting overnight stops between public land camping
resources.notes: Must be self-contained. Good way to break up long transits with unique experiences. Not public-land camping; follow host rules and arrival windows. Typically pays for itself after 2-3 nights.

resources.name: Boondockers Welcome
resources.cost: $79/year standalone; bundled in Harvest Hosts plans
resources.description: Community-driven RV network connecting travelers with private property owners offering free overnight parking. 3,421+ host locations across the U.S. and Canada. Over 70% of hosts offer electric/water hookups. Up to 2-5 night stays at most hosts.
features: 70%+ of hosts offer 15A/30A electric or water
Up to 2-5 night stays per host
resources.bestFor: Free community overnight stays with hookup potential
resources.notes: Self-contained RV required; sewer hookups are rare; generator rules vary by host. Bundled with Harvest Hosts plans.

resources.name: DeLorme Atlas & Gazetteer
resources.description: Comprehensive state-by-state topographic map books with clear markings of public lands, back roads, and recreation areas. Excellent for identifying BLM areas, remote roads, and access points. Many experienced overlanders carry these as essential backup.
resources.bestFor: Backup navigation and area exploration when digital tools fail
resources.notes: Buy for each Western state you plan to visit. Many experienced overlanders carry these as essential backup.

resources.name: Benchmark Road & Recreation Atlas
resources.description: Giant map books with comprehensive topographic maps and clear markings of public lands. Excellent for finding BLM areas, dispersed camping sites, remote back roads, and access points. Many experienced BLM campers recommend this alongside DeLorme for finding camping areas and access roads.
resources.bestFor: Detailed area exploration and public land identification
resources.notes: Many experienced BLM campers recommend this alongside DeLorme for finding camping areas and access roads.

resources.name: Caltrans QuickMap
resources.description: California real-time highway conditions, closures, chain controls, incidents, and cameras. Check before mountain passes, Sierra routes, wildfire detours, and coastal slides.
resources.bestFor: California mountain travel during winter and fire season
resources.notes: Check before mountain passes, Sierra routes, wildfire detours, and coastal slides.

resources.name: COtrip
resources.description: Colorado DOT traveler map for road conditions, closures, cameras, and restrictions. Critical for high passes, snow, rockfall, and construction delays.
resources.bestFor: Colorado mountain pass travel conditions
resources.notes: Critical for high passes, snow, rockfall, and construction delays.

resources.name: COTREX (Colorado Trail Explorer)
resources.description: Free app from Colorado Parks and Wildlife displaying trails for hiking, biking, horseback, and OHV use. Features downloadable offline maps, ride tracking, GPS capabilities, and info on seasonal closures for 41,000+ miles of trails. Colorado statewide trail map led by Colorado Parks and Wildlife with activity filters including motorcycle and OHV.
resources.bestFor: Adventure bike trail finding in Colorado
resources.notes: Free state-specific app. COTREX warns users to verify access and restrictions with the appropriate land manager. Check if other Western states have similar apps.

resources.name: Google Earth
resources.description: Satellite imagery tool for scouting potential campsites, access roads, and terrain before arrival. Switch between satellite and map views to assess road conditions and identify obstacles. After identifying BLM areas on a map, use Google Earth to visually assess terrain and road conditions before committing.
resources.bestFor: Pre-trip visual reconnaissance of dispersed camping areas and access roads
resources.notes: Essential for pre-trip visual reconnaissance of BLM terrain and road conditions.

resources.name: Backcountry Discovery Routes (BDR)
resources.cost: Free GPS tracks; paid maps/merch optional
resources.description: Non-profit organization providing free GPX tracks and maps for multi-day, off-pavement adventure motorcycle routes across Western states. Big-bike-friendly backcountry adventure motorcycle routes with GPS tracks, route pages, maps, updates, and videos. Check route-specific updates, seasonal closures, snow, fire, and washout reports.
resources.bestFor: Adventure motorcycle touring on curated backcountry routes
resources.notes: Check route-specific updates, seasonal closures, snow, fire, and washout reports.

resources.name: OsmAnd
resources.description: Fully open-source navigation app built on OpenStreetMap data. Completely offline after download with contour lines, hill-shading, hiking/bike paths, and Wikipedia POIs. Updated monthly. Great free alternative with deep customization. Offline OSM-based navigation app with GPX overlays, customizable profiles, and offline routing.
resources.bestFor: Free, fully offline topo navigation with deep customization
resources.notes: No crowd-sourced trail conditions; public land boundaries require external overlay; less polished UI than commercial apps. Potentially useful for secondary/offline routing, but confirm low-clearance data independently.

resources.name: USGS Topo Maps (topoView)
resources.description: Free USGS topographic maps available for download. GeoPDF format compatible with Avenza Maps. 7.5-minute quadrangle maps cover the entire US with detailed terrain, elevation, water features, and roads. USGS national source for topographic data, US Topo products, historical topo access, web services, and downloads.
resources.bestFor: Detailed terrain analysis for adventure bike route planning
resources.notes: Also available through Avenza Maps store. Good for planning adventure bike routes where terrain detail matters. Excellent base-map source for printing, GIS work, and offline reference.

resources.name: USGS PAD-US Data Explorer
resources.description: Official U.S. protected/open-space inventory and land-management data explorer. Good for identifying public vs protected vs access-limited lands; verify camping rules separately. Official U.S. protected areas database.
resources.bestFor: Identifying protected areas and land management designations
resources.notes: Good for identifying public vs protected vs access-limited lands; verify camping rules separately.

resources.name: LowClearances.com Route-1 Database
resources.description: Low-clearance alert database for GPS devices and mapping software. Use as an alert layer; not a substitute for RV/truck routing or signs. Low-clearance alert database with commercial-truck focus.
resources.bestFor: Supplemental low-clearance alert layer for GPS devices
resources.notes: Use as an alert layer; not a substitute for RV/truck routing or signs.

resources.name: Roadtrippers RV-Safe Routing
resources.description: Road trip planner with AI trip planner (Autopilot) that learns about your rig and plans RV-friendly itineraries. Discover attractions, campgrounds, and scenic routes along your path. Good for the big-picture itinerary. Use specialized tools for detailed navigation and camping. Road trip planner with RV-safe routing options for vehicle height, weight, and propane in premium tiers.
resources.bestFor: High-level road trip itinerary planning with points of interest
resources.notes: Use in parallel with Garmin/CoPilot and state DOT restrictions. Good for big-picture itinerary.

resources.name: NIFC National Incident Map
resources.description: National Interagency Fire Center incident mapping resource for current wildfire situational awareness. Use with state DOT closures, InciWeb, and local forest/BLM alerts. National wildfire incident mapping resource.
resources.bestFor: Current wildfire situational awareness and route planning
resources.notes: Use with state DOT closures, InciWeb, and local forest/BLM alerts.

resources.name: NASA FIRMS Fire Map
resources.description: Satellite-derived active fire/hotspot map useful for early awareness near remote routes. Hotspots are not the same as official closures; confirm with land managers and DOT. Satellite-derived active fire/hotspot map.
resources.bestFor: Early fire detection awareness for remote route planning
resources.notes: Hotspots are not the same as official closures; confirm with land managers and DOT.

resources.name: MyRadar
resources.description: Fast, lightweight weather radar app with real-time storm tracking, wind overlays, and lightning detection. Essential for daily departure decisions in the Western U.S. where afternoon thunderstorms are common in mountain terrain.
resources.bestFor: Daily go/no-go weather decisions and storm avoidance
resources.notes: Requires cell/WiFi for real-time data; no offline weather.

resources.name: Windy.com
resources.description: Stunning wind and weather visualization tool using multiple forecast models. Essential for understanding wind patterns, mountain pass conditions, and fire weather in the Western U.S. Displays wind, rain, temperature, and smoke/aerosol layers. Complex UI for new users.
features: Multiple forecast model support (ECMWF, GFS, ICON)
Mountain pass wind and temperature forecasts
Smoke and aerosol layer (critical near wildfires)
resources.bestFor: Mountain pass planning, wind assessment, and wildfire smoke monitoring
resources.notes: Requires internet; complex UI for new users. Essential for mountain passes and wildfire smoke.

resources.name: Garmin inReach Mini 2
resources.description: Two-way Iridium satellite communicator for backcountry and remote travel. Send/receive text messages, share GPS location, and trigger 24/7 emergency SOS — all without cell service. Pairs with Zumo XT2 for integrated navigation + emergency capability. Two-way Iridium satellite communicator for backcountry and remote travel.
resources.bestFor: Off-grid emergency communication for solo moto and remote RV travel
resources.notes: Monthly satellite plan required; message delays in heavy tree cover. For off-grid emergency communication.

resources.name: USFS MVUM ArcGIS Feature Layer
resources.description: Authoritative USFS enterprise GIS feature layer with 28,746 motorized vehicle use records. Includes per-route data on allowed vehicle classes, seasonal windows, trail class, e-bike categories, and district info. Data updated January 30, 2025. Free CC0 license for any use. For developers or power users building custom map tools or integrations.
features: Seasonal date windows per vehicle class
resources.bestFor: Developers or power users building custom map tools or integrations
resources.notes: Requires GIS software or ArcGIS Online to use; not a consumer app. For advanced users.

resources.name: BLM Geospatial Data / GBP Hub
resources.description: Central place to discover, view, and download BLM geospatial data. Useful for building your own GIS layers or importing into QGIS/ArcGIS workflows. Central BLM map page with National Data viewer, recreation map, georeferenced PDFs, and downloadable map products.
resources.bestFor: Power users wanting raw BLM data for custom mapping
resources.notes: Useful for building your own GIS layers or importing into QGIS/ArcGIS workflows.

resources.name: USFS Enterprise Data Warehouse / Geodata
resources.description: Authoritative Forest Service data access including map services, data downloads, and ArcGIS resources. Useful for advanced route planning and building custom map overlays. Authoritative Forest Service data access.
resources.bestFor: Advanced route planning and building custom map overlays
resources.notes: Useful for advanced route planning and building custom map overlays.

resources.name: Recreation Information Database API
resources.description: Programmatic access to federal recreation areas, facilities, campsites, tours, and permits. Useful if you later want to automate campground imports into your own database. Programmatic access to federal recreation data.
resources.bestFor: Developers wanting federal recreation data integration
resources.notes: Useful if you want to automate campground imports into your own database.

resources.name: National Park Service App
resources.description: Official NPS app with park information, interactive maps, accessibility info, and offline park downloads. Download park content before arriving; cell service is often poor inside western parks. Official NPS mobile app.
resources.bestFor: National park visits with offline maps and info
resources.notes: Download park content before arriving; cell service is often poor inside western parks.

resources.name: Boondockers Bible
resources.description: Free web-based boondocking map and database with crowd-sourced reviews from experienced boondockers. Provides honest feedback on road conditions, crowding, cell reception by carrier, and nearby dump stations. No account required. Free web-based boondocking map and database.
resources.bestFor: Pre-trip boondocking site research with real-world conditions
resources.notes: Web-only; no offline maps; requires cell/WiFi to access.

resources.name: KampTrail
resources.description: Uses verified federal campsite data from Recreation.gov RIDB API rather than user-submitted content. Fills the gap left by FreeRoam's shutdown in 2024. Built using verified federal campsite data from the Recreation.gov RIDB API.
resources.bestFor: Finding federal campsites with verified official data
resources.notes: Newer tool built after FreeRoam shut down in 2024. Uses official Recreation.gov data.

resources.name: Boondocking.org
resources.description: User-submitted dispersed camping spots with GPS pins. Community-driven database of free camping locations. Use as supplemental source alongside Campendium and iOverlander. User-submitted dispersed camping spots.
resources.bestFor: Supplemental source for dispersed camping locations
resources.notes: Use as supplemental source alongside Campendium and iOverlander.

resources.name: Hammer Truck GPS
resources.description: Truck-safe routing app that advertises low bridge and truck-restriction avoidance. Designed for trucks; adapt carefully for RV use. Truck-safe routing app that advertises low bridge and truck-restriction avoidance.
resources.bestFor: Supplemental truck-style RV routing
resources.notes: Designed for trucks; adapt carefully for RV use.

resources.name: TruckMap
resources.description: Truck-oriented GPS app with custom height/weight and low-clearance restriction routing claims. Commercial-truck focus; compare routes with RV-specific tools. Truck-oriented GPS app.
resources.bestFor: Supplemental truck-style routing with height awareness
resources.notes: Commercial-truck focus; compare routes with RV-specific tools.

resources.name: Trailforks
resources.description: Largest crowd-sourced trail database in the world. Excellent for finding and navigating established single-track and OHV trails. User-submitted conditions and heat maps show current trail status. Pro required for offline use. Largest crowd-sourced trail database.
resources.bestFor: Finding and navigating established OHV and dual-sport trails
resources.notes: Limited backcountry/dispersed coverage; basic map layers; cannot create custom routes easily. Pro required for offline use.

resources.name: Togo RV Navigator (Roadpass Pro)
resources.description: Simple RV-safe GPS navigator using your rig's dimensions for offline routing. Integrated into the broader Togo RV / Roadpass Pro ecosystem alongside campground search and roadside assistance. Best value only if you use the broader Roadpass Pro ecosystem.
features: Part of Roadpass Pro membership suite
resources.bestFor: RVers already in the Roadpass/Togo ecosystem wanting integrated nav
resources.notes: Best value only if you use the broader Roadpass Pro ecosystem.

resources.name: USGS topoBuilder
resources.description: Create custom USGS topographic maps from The National Map data. Use for custom map sheets around planned camp zones or riding loops. Custom USGS topo map builder.
resources.bestFor: Creating custom topo maps for specific areas
resources.notes: Use for custom map sheets around planned camp zones or riding loops.

resources.name: Public Lands of the West Recreation Map
resources.description: Interactive map featuring recreation sites across public lands of the western United States. Partnership between Public Lands Interpretive Association, USFS, and BLM. Covers all 11 Western states. Online store offers physical BLM and Forest Service maps.
resources.bestFor: Overview research of recreation opportunities across Western public lands
resources.notes: Covers all 11 Western states. Online store offers physical BLM and Forest Service maps.

resources.name: BLM Recreation Site Locator
resources.description: Official BLM camping information including developed campgrounds, dispersed camping guidelines, and Long-Term Visitor Areas in CA and AZ. Covers rules, stay limits, and facility information. BLM manages Long-Term Visitor Areas in CA and AZ where winter visitors can camp up to 7 months with a permit.
resources.bestFor: Official BLM camping rules and developed campground locations
resources.notes: BLM manages Long-Term Visitor Areas in CA and AZ where winter visitors can camp up to 7 months with a permit.

resources.name: Stay The Trail (Colorado MVUMs)
resources.description: Central portal for all USFS Motor Vehicle Use Maps for Colorado National Forests and Grasslands. Maps kept up to date and all available through Avenza map store for free. Model example of a state MVUM portal. Other states may have similar consolidated resources.
resources.bestFor: One-stop download of all Colorado forest MVUMs
resources.notes: Model example of a state MVUM portal. Check for similar resources in other states.

resources.name: Overland Bound One
resources.description: Crowdsourced overlanding community with camping information from local experts. Includes BLM routes, USFS MVUM roads and trails, topo maps, and satellite imagery. Expedition mode offers offline navigation, track recording, and GPX import. Check their forums for downloadable route sections. Expedition mode membership required for full offline capability.
resources.bestFor: Community-driven route discovery and trip planning
resources.notes: Check their forums for downloadable route sections. Expedition mode membership required for full offline capability.

resources.name: Rever Motorcycle App + Butler Maps
resources.cost: Free / Pro (subscription); Butler Maps overlay free in-app
resources.description: Dedicated motorcycle trip planner and GPS app with 3,000+ curated road maps worldwide. Integrates Butler Maps rated roads (G1 epic/ADV dirt/Paved Trails) as an in-app overlay. Offline maps, turn-by-turn navigation, voice guidance, and live friend tracking.
features: Butler Maps overlay: G1, ADV, Paved Trails, Lost Highways
resources.bestFor: End-to-end adventure moto trip planning and on-bike navigation
resources.notes: Best features may require Pro subscription; verify current pricing.

resources.name: Butler Maps (Physical + Digital)
resources.cost: Physical maps ~$15–$25 each; digital via Rever
resources.description: Waterproof, tear-resistant curated motorcycle maps for the Western U.S. Each map highlights the best paved (G1 rated) and dirt (ADV rated) roads. Adventure Series covers AZ, CO, ID, NM, Southern CA, UT, and WY. Also available as digital overlays in the Rever app.
features: Adventure Series: AZ, CO, ID, NM, SoCal, UT, WY
resources.bestFor: Discovering the best motorcycle roads in the Western U.S.
resources.notes: Physical maps require manual navigation; digital requires Rever app.

resources.name: Garmin Zumo XT2
resources.description: Garmin's flagship dedicated motorcycle GPS with a 6-inch glove-friendly HD touchscreen. IP65 waterproof and shockproof. Includes topographic maps, BirdsEye Satellite imagery (no subscription), adventurous routing, and group ride tracking via Tread app. Purpose-built, all-weather dedicated moto GPS for off-road and touring.
features: Group Ride via Garmin Tread app
resources.bestFor: Purpose-built, all-weather dedicated moto GPS for off-road and touring
resources.notes: Expensive; touchscreen can be temperamental; best paired with Tread app.

resources.name: Scenic Motorcycle App
resources.description: iOS motorcycle navigation app optimized for use while riding. Clean, minimal HUD-style interface, GPX route import, offline maps, and CarPlay support. Popular among riders who plan routes in MyRoute-app or CalTopo then want best-in-class on-bike UX. iOS motorcycle navigation app optimized for use while riding.
resources.bestFor: iOS users wanting the cleanest on-bike navigation experience
resources.notes: iOS only; no Android version.

resources.name: MyRoute-app (MRA)
resources.description: Precision route planning tool for multi-day motorcycle trips. Allows detailed day-by-day itinerary creation, waypoint management, and GPX/KML export to navigation apps like Scenic or Garmin Zumo. Strong preference among touring riders for pre-trip planning. Best used as a planning tool, not real-time navigation.
features: Import into Scenic, Zumo, and other nav apps
resources.bestFor: Pre-trip multi-day route planning with export to on-bike nav apps
resources.notes: Best used as a planning tool, not real-time navigation.

resources.name: Kurviger
resources.description: Route planner and navigation app optimized to maximize twisty, scenic roads and avoid highways. Great for U.S. mountain and canyon riding. GPX export for use in other apps. Android-only for mobile; less U.S. road data quality than Europe.
resources.bestFor: Generating maximally twisty routes through mountain and canyon terrain
resources.notes: Android-only for mobile; less U.S. road data quality than Europe.

resources.name: TransAmerica Trail
resources.description: Long-distance cross-country adventure route navigable with maps, roll charts, or GPS tracks. Confirm track currency and local closures before committing to remote sections. Long-distance cross-country adventure route.
resources.bestFor: Cross-country adventure motorcycle travel
resources.notes: Confirm track currency and local closures before committing to remote sections.

resources.name: Adventure Cycling Interactive Network Map
resources.cost: Free preview; paid route products vary
resources.description: Long-distance bicycle and bikepacking route network, including western routes such as Great Divide and Western Express. Useful crossover reference for remote corridors, services, and elevation planning.
resources.bestFor: Reference for remote corridors and services on dual-sport friendly routes
resources.notes: Useful crossover reference for remote corridors, services, and elevation planning.

resources.name: AZ511
resources.description: Arizona traveler information for incidents, closures, construction, cameras, and travel times. Pair with ADOT alerts during monsoon, dust, and wildfire events. Arizona 511 system.
resources.bestFor: Arizona road conditions and closures
resources.notes: Pair with ADOT alerts during monsoon, dust, and wildfire events.

resources.name: Idaho 511
resources.description: Idaho road condition and traveler information system. Useful for mountain routes, winter closures, and remote highway conditions. Idaho 511 traveler map.
resources.bestFor: Idaho mountain and highway travel conditions
resources.notes: Useful for mountain routes, winter closures, and remote highway conditions.

resources.name: Montana 511
resources.description: Montana traveler map for road conditions, incidents, road work, restrictions, rest areas, cameras, and weather. Important for wind, winter, and long rural distances. Montana 511 system.
resources.bestFor: Montana highway travel conditions
resources.notes: Important for wind, winter, and long rural distances.

resources.name: NMRoads
resources.description: Official road advisory system for New Mexico road conditions, closures, advisories, and traffic information. Reported conditions are limited to NMDOT-maintained routes. New Mexico road advisory system.
resources.bestFor: New Mexico road conditions and closures
resources.notes: Reported conditions are limited to NMDOT-maintained routes.

resources.name: Nevada 511 / NVRoads
resources.description: Nevada traveler information for road conditions, closures, and traffic events. Use before crossing remote basins or snow-prone ranges. Nevada 511 system.
resources.bestFor: Nevada desert and mountain road conditions
resources.notes: Use before crossing remote basins or snow-prone ranges.

resources.name: Oregon TripCheck
resources.description: Oregon traveler information for road/weather conditions, closures, construction, snow zones, and cameras. Very useful for Cascades, Coast Range, Columbia River Gorge, and eastern Oregon. Oregon TripCheck system.
resources.bestFor: Oregon mountain pass and coastal travel conditions
resources.notes: Very useful for Cascades, Coast Range, Columbia River Gorge, and eastern Oregon.

resources.name: UDOT Traffic
resources.description: Utah traffic and road-condition system with crashes, construction, road weather, delays, and cameras. Check for canyon closures, seasonal roads, and national park gateway congestion. Utah DOT traffic system.
resources.bestFor: Utah canyon and pass road conditions
resources.notes: Check for canyon closures, seasonal roads, and national park gateway congestion.

resources.name: WSDOT Travel Map
resources.description: Washington real-time travel map with alerts, cameras, route times, mountain passes, and weather alerts. Essential for Cascades pass travel and wildfire/snow closures. Washington DOT travel map.
resources.bestFor: Washington pass travel and wildfire/snow closures
resources.notes: Essential for Cascades pass travel and wildfire/snow closures.

resources.name: WYDOT WyoRoad / Wyoming 511
resources.description: WYDOT road and travel information for closures, restrictions, advisories, cameras, and weather. Critical for wind closures, winter gates, and high-elevation routes. Wyoming 511 system.
resources.bestFor: Wyoming wind closures and winter gate conditions
resources.notes: Critical for wind closures, winter gates, and high-elevation routes.

resources.name: Arizona State Parks OHV Program
resources.cost: Free information; permit/decal fees may apply
resources.description: Arizona State Parks OHV program information, safety, decals, grants, and riding resources. Use with BLM/USFS travel maps for legal route confirmation. Arizona OHV program.
resources.bestFor: Arizona OHV regulations and permit information
resources.notes: Use with BLM/USFS travel maps for legal route confirmation.

resources.name: California OHMVR Division
resources.description: California State Parks Off-Highway Motor Vehicle Recreation Division portal for OHV parks, maps, safety, and regulations. Check SVRA rules, closures, fire restrictions, and sound/spark-arrestor requirements. California OHV portal.
resources.bestFor: California OHV park and regulation information
resources.notes: Check SVRA rules, closures, fire restrictions, and sound/spark-arrestor requirements.

resources.name: Idaho Trails / IDPR OHV Resources
resources.description: Idaho trail and OHV mapping resources linked with IDPR off-highway vehicle guidance. Cross-check with USFS MVUM, BLM travel maps, and Idaho Department of Lands recreation maps. Idaho trail and OHV mapping.
resources.bestFor: Idaho OHV trail discovery and regulations
resources.notes: Cross-check with USFS MVUM, BLM travel maps, and Idaho Department of Lands recreation maps.

resources.name: Montana FWP Off-Highway Vehicles
resources.cost: Free information; trail pass may apply
resources.description: Montana OHV registration/trail-pass information and guidance directing riders to local USFS/BLM maps. Montana motorized access is highly dependent on the managing agency and local travel plan. Montana OHV information.
resources.bestFor: Montana OHV permit and regulation information
resources.notes: Montana motorized access is highly dependent on the managing agency and local travel plan.

resources.name: Montana Recreation Maps
resources.description: Montana recreation mapping site including summer motorized trail mapping. Use as a discovery tool; verify with official USFS/BLM MVUM/travel maps. Montana recreation mapping.
resources.bestFor: Montana recreation and OHV trail discovery
resources.notes: Use as a discovery tool; verify with official USFS/BLM MVUM/travel maps.

resources.name: Off-Road Nevada Trails
resources.description: Nevada Off-Highway Vehicles trail map and planning portal. Remote Nevada routes require fuel/water planning and current closure/fire checks. Nevada OHV trail portal.
resources.bestFor: Nevada OHV trail discovery and planning
resources.notes: Remote Nevada routes require fuel/water planning and current closure/fire checks.

resources.name: New Mexico OHV Program
resources.description: New Mexico OHV program information and responsible recreation resources. Use with BLM and USFS local route maps, especially around mixed land ownership. New Mexico OHV program.
resources.bestFor: New Mexico OHV regulations and permits
resources.notes: Use with BLM and USFS local route maps, especially around mixed land ownership.

resources.name: Oregon Parks and Recreation ATV Places to Ride
resources.description: Oregon riding areas and ATV/OHV guide information, including designated areas and map references. Many Oregon OHV maps can be saved as PDFs for offline field use. Oregon OHV riding guide.
resources.bestFor: Oregon ATV and OHV riding area discovery
resources.notes: Many Oregon OHV maps can be saved as PDFs for offline field use.

resources.name: Oregon Department of Forestry OHV Trails
resources.description: Oregon state forest OHV trail information and maps, including Tillamook State Forest riding areas. Check seasonal fire restrictions and trail-condition reports before riding. Oregon state forest OHV trails.
resources.bestFor: Oregon state forest OHV trail riding
resources.notes: Check seasonal fire restrictions and trail-condition reports before riding.

resources.name: Utah OHV Maps and Trails
resources.description: Utah Division of Outdoor Recreation OHV maps page with online/offline map resources and land-agency links. Utah emphasizes riding only on designated routes open by sign or approved map. Utah OHV maps portal.
resources.bestFor: Utah OHV trail maps and riding information
resources.notes: Utah emphasizes riding only on designated routes open by sign or approved map.

resources.name: Washington DNR Recreation Map
resources.cost: Free information; Discover Pass may apply
resources.description: Washington DNR recreation map and site information for trails, campgrounds, day-use areas, and ORV areas. Check Discover Pass, daily alerts, gates, and active timber operations. Washington DNR recreation map.
resources.bestFor: Washington DNR land recreation and ORV areas
resources.notes: Check Discover Pass, daily alerts, gates, and active timber operations.

resources.name: Wyoming GIS Maps / State Parks ORV and OSV Maps
resources.description: Wyoming GIS office page linking State Parks ORV/OSV maps created with USFS for trail maps across Wyoming. Confirm current ORV permit requirements and USFS MVUM designations. Wyoming GIS/ORV maps.
resources.bestFor: Wyoming ORV and OSV trail maps
resources.notes: Confirm current ORV permit requirements and USFS MVUM designations.

resources.name: WildernessConnect
resources.description: Service of University of Montana with information on all wilderness areas in the country. Interactive map to locate wilderness areas. Note: motorized vehicles are prohibited in wilderness areas. IMPORTANT: Motorized vehicles are PROHIBITED in wilderness areas. Know the boundaries.
resources.bestFor: Identifying wilderness boundaries to AVOID with motorized vehicles
resources.notes: IMPORTANT: Motorized vehicles are PROHIBITED in wilderness areas. Know the boundaries to avoid violations.

resources.name: US Fish & Wildlife Service Interactive Map
resources.description: Locate National Wildlife Refuges, USFWS offices, and other managed lands. Some refuges allow camping and have scenic driving routes suitable for motorhomes. Some NWRs have excellent camping and wildlife viewing opportunities.
resources.bestFor: Identifying wildlife refuges along your route for camping and scenic drives
resources.notes: Some NWRs have excellent camping and wildlife viewing opportunities.

resources.name: BLM Visit / Recreation Search
resources.description: Search BLM recreation sites and activities, including camping and OHV opportunities. Good starting point before calling the local field office. BLM recreation search map.
resources.bestFor: BLM recreation site discovery and research
resources.notes: Good starting point before calling the local field office.

resources.name: BLM Camping on Public Lands
resources.description: BLM overview for developed campgrounds, dispersed camping, stay limits, and local-rule reminders. Rules vary by field office, conservation area, fire restriction, and posted order. BLM camping overview.
resources.bestFor: Understanding BLM camping rules and regulations
resources.notes: Rules vary by field office, conservation area, fire restriction, and posted order.

resources.name: USFS Geospatial Data Discovery Hub
resources.description: Open data site where the public can explore Forest Service geospatial datasets by category. Build custom maps and applications using official USFS data. Includes interactive tools and ArcGIS applications. For advanced users. Scroll to 'More Applications' section for curated interactive tools.
resources.bestFor: Power users who want raw USFS data for custom mapping
resources.notes: For advanced users. Scroll to 'More Applications' section for curated interactive tools.
